dataset.query: FROM logs-azure.data_factory* | WHERE azure.data_factory.activity_name == "pipeline_complete" | STATS `Total runs` = COUNT()

FROM logs-azure.data_factory* | WHERE azure.data_factory.activity_name == "pipeline_complete" | STATS total = COUNT(), failures = COUNT_IF(azure.data_factory.status == "Failed") | EVAL `Failure %` = ROUND(failures * 100.0 / total, 1)

FROM logs-azure.data_factory* | WHERE azure.data_factory.activity_name == "pipeline_complete" AND azure.data_factory.duration_ms IS NOT NULL | STATS `Avg duration` = ROUND(AVG(azure.data_factory.duration_ms), 0)
FROM logs-azure.data_factory* | WHERE azure.data_factory.activity_name == "pipeline_complete" AND azure.data_factory.quality_check == "DEGRADED" | STATS `Degraded` = COUNT()

config.title: Pipeline events over time by service

dataset.query: FROM logs-azure.* | WHERE labels.pipeline_run_id IS NOT NULL | EVAL svc = COALESCE(cloud.service.name, "unknown") | STATS count = COUNT() BY svc, bucket = DATE_TRUNC(30 minutes, @timestamp)
FROM logs-azure.* | WHERE labels.pipeline_run_id IS NOT NULL | STATS count = COUNT() BY event.outcome

config.title: Databricks Spark — records read vs written

dataset.query: FROM logs-azure.databricks* | WHERE azure.databricks.spark.records_read IS NOT NULL | STATS read = SUM(azure.databricks.spark.records_read), written = SUM(azure.databricks.spark.records_written) BY bucket = DATE_TRUNC(30 minutes, @timestamp)
FROM logs-azure.synapse* | WHERE azure.synapse.data_processed_bytes IS NOT NULL | STATS bytes = SUM(azure.synapse.data_processed_bytes) BY bucket = DATE_TRUNC(30 minutes, @timestamp)
FROM logs-azure.* | WHERE labels.pipeline_run_id IS NOT NULL AND event.outcome == "failure" | STATS count = COUNT() BY error.type, bucket = DATE_TRUNC(30 minutes, @timestamp)
FROM logs-azure.blob_storage* | WHERE labels.pipeline_run_id IS NOT NULL | STATS bytes = SUM(azure.blob_storage.content_length) BY azure.blob_storage.operation_name

FROM logs-azure.data_factory* | WHERE azure.data_factory.activity_name == "pipeline_complete" | KEEP @timestamp, azure.data_factory.pipeline_name, azure.data_factory.status, azure.data_factory.duration_ms, azure.data_factory.quality_check, azure.data_factory.records_processed, cloud.region | SORT @timestamp DESC | LIMIT 20
FROM logs-azure.purview* | WHERE azure.purview.assets_discovered IS NOT NULL | STATS discovered = SUM(azure.purview.assets_discovered) BY bucket = DATE_TRUNC(30 minutes, @timestamp)
